grantTitle: COP-PILOT Open Call #1 - CL2 Smart Sustainable IoT Solutions (Valencia)
programName: COP-PILOT (Collaborative Open Platform for PILOTing services across emerging smart IoT and Edge computing environments)
summary: COP-PILOT Open Call #1 offers up to EUR 200,000 per project (100% funding) for SME-led innovations in smart sustainable IoT solutions for Valencia, Spain. The call targets 8 projects across multiple clusters, with Cluster 2 (CL2) focusing on smart cities, urban development, environmental monitoring, and IoT platform integration. Projects must integrate with the COP-PILOT platform and demonstrate TRL progression from 6 to 7-8 over an 8-month execution period.
objectives: Develop and pilot innovative IoT solutions that integrate with the COP-PILOT Collaborative Open Platform framework
Demonstrate end-to-end orchestration of services across diverse edge computing, 5G connectivity, and decentralized processing environments
Address specific challenges in Valencia's smart city infrastructure including smart access/resource management, robotic surveillance, autonomous transport, and municipal building automation
Validate COP-PILOT platform components (ServOrch, SIF, LLM-UI) through real-world deployment in Valencia cluster environments
Progress technology readiness from TRL 5-6 to TRL 7-8 while ensuring compliance with open standards (FIWARE, TMForum APIs, NGSI-LD, Eclipse Arrowhead)
Support Valencia's carbon neutrality goals by 2030 through sustainable smart city IoT innovations
Demonstrate cross-sector scenarios and replication potential beyond the initial deployment

requirements.description: Lead applicant MUST be an SME registered in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe Associated Country
requirements.evidence: Company registration documents proving SME status and EU/Associated Country registration

requirements.description: Eligible as single SME or SME-led consortium of at least 2 partners (no maximum). Other partners can be SMEs, large enterprises, research institutions, universities, non-profit organizations, or public entities
requirements.evidence: Consortium agreement if multi-partner; partner eligibility documentation

requirements.description: Organization must not be bankrupt, subject to insolvency proceedings, guilty of grave professional misconduct or fraud, and must be compliant with tax and social security obligations
requirements.evidence: Legal declarations and certificates of good standing

requirements.description: Proposal must be submitted in English only, maximum 12 pages
requirements.evidence: English-language proposal document within page limit

requirements.description: Submission through https://cop-pilot.eu/ between 03 March 2026 09:00 CET and 04 May 2026 22:00 CET (strict deadline, late submissions rejected)
requirements.evidence: Online submission confirmation

requirements.description: Maximum funding request of EUR 200,000 per project
requirements.evidence: Detailed budget breakdown not exceeding EUR 200,000

requirements.description: Indirect costs limited to maximum 25% of direct costs
requirements.evidence: Budget table showing indirect costs calculation

requirements.description: Subcontracting limited to maximum 20% of total budget with clear justification
requirements.evidence: Subcontracting justification and cost breakdown

requirements.description: Cannot receive funding from another EU source for the same work (no double funding)
requirements.evidence: Declaration of no double funding

requirements.description: Solution must address at least one of the four CL2 Open Call Challenges: OC#2.1 (Smart Access/Resource Management at UPV), OC#2.2 (Robotic Patrol/Surveillance), OC#2.3 (Autonomous Terminal Shuttle at Port), or OC#2.4 (Municipal Buildings Management)
requirements.evidence: Clear identification and technical approach to specific challenge

requirements.description: Application must be containerized (Docker, Kubernetes) and expose performance metrics for COP-PILOT platform integration
requirements.evidence: Technical architecture showing containerization approach

requirements.description: Compliance with open standards: TMForum APIs, ETSI ZSM, NGSI-LD/NGSI-v2, FIWARE, Eclipse Arrowhead, CNCF SPIFFE, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect
requirements.evidence: Technical specification showing standards compliance

requirements.description: Integration with COP-PILOT platform components through one of two workflows: (1) Onboarding New Services via git-based repository with CI pipeline, or (2) Integrating New Infrastructure Domains via portal registration with Domain Orchestrator
requirements.evidence: Integration architecture and workflow description

requirements.description: Solution must start at minimum TRL 6 and demonstrate progression to TRL 7-8 by project end
requirements.evidence: TRL assessment at start and planned progression roadmap

requirements.description: Solutions must be compatible with cluster infrastructure: 5G/6G networks, LoRaWAN, edge computing resources, existing sensor networks, FIWARE Thinking Cities platform
requirements.evidence: Technical compatibility analysis with cluster infrastructure

requirements.description: GDPR compliance and data protection regulations adherence
requirements.evidence: Data protection impact assessment and GDPR compliance plan

requirements.description: Project duration: 8 months execution + 2 months reporting (10 months total)
requirements.evidence: Project timeline with Gantt chart

requirements.description: Mandatory milestones: M4 intermediate report and M9 final deliverable
requirements.evidence: Milestone schedule in project plan

requirements.description: Clear project phases: Design, Implementation, Testing, Evaluation/Reporting
requirements.evidence: Phased work plan

requirements.description: Open access obligations: publish findings, share code on GitHub/GitLab, acknowledge EU funding (Grant Agreement 101189819)
requirements.evidence: Open access and dissemination plan

requirements.description: Maintain financial records for 6 years after project end
requirements.evidence: Record-keeping procedures

requirements.description: Minimum score of 3 (Good) in each evaluation criterion; overall threshold of 60 points total
requirements.evidence: Proposal must meet quality thresholds in Excellence, Impact, and Implementation

evaluationCriteria.name: Excellence
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of objectives, technical approach, standards compliance, state-of-the-art analysis, and TRL progression
keyIndicators: SMART objectives and clear problem statement
Alignment with COP-PILOT goals and CL2 cluster objectives
Sound technical approach using FIWARE, TMForum APIs, Eclipse Arrowhead, GAIA-X compliance
Standards compliance (MQTT, OPC UA, REST integration protocols)
State-of-the-art analysis with novel approach beyond existing solutions
TRL progression from 5-6 to 7-8 with clear pathway
Technical innovation and feasibility of proposed solution

evaluationCriteria.name: Impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of cluster alignment, platform integration, cross-sector potential, and sustainability/dissemination plans
keyIndicators: Specific CL2 cluster and use case targeting
Clear COP-PILOT platform integration description
Effective leveraging of platform components (ServOrch, SIF, LLM-UI)
Cross-sector scenarios supported and replication potential
Comprehensive sustainability and dissemination plans
Open access and data sharing commitments
Environmental, economic and technological impact analysis
Scalability and transferability to other contexts

evaluationCriteria.name: Implementation
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of project planning, team qualifications, timeline realism, and budget justification
keyIndicators: Clear project phases: Design, Implementation, Testing, Evaluation/Reporting
Mandatory milestones at M4 (intermediate report) and M9 (final deliverable)
Realistic 8-month timeline with detailed Gantt chart
Team qualifications and relevant track record
Budget ≤ EUR 200,000 with justified breakdown by category
Indirect costs ≤ 25% of direct costs
Risk management and mitigation strategies
Quality assurance and project management approach

categories.description: Salaries for project staff directly working on the project
rules: Must be justified based on actual work performed
Maximum EUR 1,000 per person per month
Must be directly related to project activities
Only depreciation costs eligible (not full purchase price)
Must be justified for project needs
Must be directly attributable to the project

categories.description: Overhead costs not directly attributable to project
rules: No additional justification required beyond percentage

targetBeneficiaries: SMEs (Small and Medium Enterprises) as lead applicants
SME-led consortia including large enterprises, research institutions, universities, non-profit organizations, and public entities
Technology providers specializing in IoT, edge computing, 5G/6G connectivity, smart city solutions
Service providers in urban development, environmental monitoring, building management, autonomous systems
Infrastructure owners and operators in smart cities
Innovation actors addressing Valencia's carbon neutrality goals
geographicScope: Valencia, Spain (City of Valencia, Port of Valencia, Industrial Park Almussafes, UPV Campus Vera)
eligibleCountries: Horizon Europe Associated Countries (including Iceland, Norway, Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, North Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, Turkey, Israel, Moldova, Ukraine, Tunisia, Georgia, Armenia, Kosovo)
keyThemes: Open standards and interoperability (FIWARE, TMForum, NGSI-LD)
additionalNotes: COP-PILOT is funded under EU HORIZON programme (Grant Agreement 101189819) under call HORIZON-CL4-2024-DATA-01, topic HORIZON-CL4-2024-DATA-01-03
Total call budget is EUR 1,600,000 expecting to fund 8 projects (1-2 per cluster), with CL2 being one of multiple clusters
100% funding rate means no co-financing required from applicants
Payment schedule: 50% upon contract signature (July 2026), 50% upon final report approval (2027)
Valencia was European Green Capital 2024 and aims for carbon neutrality by 2030, providing strong policy alignment

Four specific Open Call Challenges for CL2: OC#2.1 (Smart Access/Resource Management at UPV Campus), OC#2.2 (Robotic Patrol/Surveillance), OC#2.3 (Autonomous Terminal Shuttle at Port), OC#2.4 (Municipal Buildings Management with closed-loop automation)

Extensive infrastructure available: private 5G networks (n40, n78, n258), LoRaWAN networks, edge computing resources (HPE ProLiant, Dell PowerEdge servers), FIWARE Thinking Cities platform by Telefonica

CL2 has operational use cases already deployed: 5G radars for traffic/flood monitoring, IoT waste management sensors, maritime traffic monitoring, smart building management at La Harinera, environmental quality monitoring
Applicants can either deploy new infrastructure domain OR leverage existing CL2 domains (UPV Campus or Valencia Port)
Technical documentation available at: https://thinking-cities.readthedocs.io and https://github.com/telefonicaid
COP-PILOT platform uses modern tech stack: Next.js, React, Keycloak, Apache Kafka, Kubernetes, OpenZiti, NGSI-LD Context Brokers
Critical need identified for OC#2.4: closed-loop automation layer that translates building analytics into direct operational impact (HVAC control, equipment scheduling, anomaly detection)
Cross-site interconnection exists between UPV Lab and Fundacion Valenciaport via dedicated fiber
Proposals must demonstrate progress beyond state-of-the-art and analyze environmental, economic and technological impact
Integration protocols required: MQTT, OPC UA, REST APIs
Security requirements: OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, mTLS, X.509/PKI, CNCF SPIFFE compliance
Open access obligations include publishing on GitHub/GitLab and acknowledging EU funding in all materials
Threshold requirements: minimum score 3 (Good) in each of three criteria AND overall minimum 60 points (out of 100 possible)
